A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM CAUSED A SERIES OF MICROBURSTS BETWEEN CANAAN, NY AND RICHMOND, MA BETWEEN 340 PM AND 350 PM. DAMAGE BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF NY ROUTES 22 AND 295 WHERE THE DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS REPORTED A REMOTE AREA WHERE APPROXIMATELY 50 TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. ANOTHER LARGE MICROBURST OCCURRED IN THE TOWN OF RICHMOND WHERE SEVEN ROADS WERE CLOSED DUE TO DOWNED TREES. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75 AND 85 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH VARYING FROM 10 YARDS TO UP TO A MILE WIDE.

A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED MICROBURSTS NEAR WAPPINGERS FALLS. TWO MAIN AREAS OF DAMAGE WERE NOTED. THE FIRST WAS AT BOWDOIN PARK ALONG THE HUDSON RIVER WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED. THE SECOND WAS NEAR MESIER PARK WHERE APPROXIMATELY 50 TREES WERE DOWNED WITH SOME FALLING ON HOMES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AND AT LEAST ONE POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75 AND 85 MPH WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES AND WIDTH OF 275 YARDS.
